humanSummary.text: UTSUSHI-030 delivers the conformance ingestion seam end-to-end: a single TS schema mirror (`packages/localization-bridge-schema/src/conformance.ts`, 893 LOC) reproduces the Rust validator's structural rules under `CONFORMANCE_SCHEMA_VERSION_V01 = "0.2.0-alpha"`; a single DB migration (`packages/itotori-db/migrations/0028_runtime_conformance_results.sql`, 161 LOC) lands the four expected tables (`itotori_conformance_runs`, `_results`, `_evidence_refs`, `_findings`) with text-typed tier columns and the CHECK-constraint belt-and-suspenders the plan specified; a repository, a `ProjectWorkflow.ingestConformanceReport` service entry, a CLI subcommand, and 4 positive + 6 negative fixtures land alongside. All six acceptance criteria are satisfied. (1) Whitelist enforcement is layered: `SEMANTIC_CODE_PATTERN = /^(utsushi|kaifuu)\.[a-z][a-z0-9_]*\.[a-z][a-z0-9_]*$/u` plus a strict-prefix gate `["utsushi.conformance.", "utsushi.snapshot.", "kaifuu."]` enforced inside `assertSemanticCodeAllowedV01` (`conformance.ts:91-98, 476-501`); test `rejects_semantic_code_outside_whitelist()` pins `itotori.conformance.semantic_code_not_allowed` against `utsushi.sink.unsupported_kind`; DB CHECK `itotori_conformance_results_semantic_code_prefix_check` (`0028_*.sql:71-80`) repeats the whitelist as `LIKE` predicates. (2) Skip / Unsupported never promote to Pass: the TS validator routes through tagged-union dispatch in `assertOutcome` (`conformance.ts:556-617`) using `assertAllowedKeys` to forbid stray `semanticCode` on `pass` (test `rejects_skip_shape_in_pass_envelope()` expects `itotori.conformance.unknown_field`); DB CHECKs `outcome_kind_check`, `pass_tier_check`, and `semantic_code_check` (`0028_*.sql:55-67`) enforce the same invariant at the storage layer. (3) Byte-equal evidence tier preserved verbatim: the `pass_evidence_tier` column is `text`, the repository never coerces, the workflow propagates `result.outcome.evidenceTier` straight through, and the headline DB integration test `conformance_repository_round_trips_a_pass_result_with_evidence_tier_byte_equal` (`conformance-repository.test.ts:106-147`) asserts `===` equality against the source enum. (4) Schema-version pin parity: TS parity test reads `crates/utsushi-core/src/conformance/mod.rs` as text and asserts the literal `pub const CONFORMANCE_SCHEMA_VERSION: &str = "0.2.0-alpha";` matches `CONFORMANCE_SCHEMA_VERSION_V01` (`conformance.test.ts:33-43`); DB migration carries `schema_version_check` enforcing the same string. (5) Engine neutrality: the validator accepts six profile ids as kebab-case strings; no engine-specific fields. (6) Evidence ref taxonomy: all seven kinds (six from UTSUSHI-026 plus `statePath` from UTSUSHI-028) round-trip through the polymorphic table with the NULL-column shape the plan committed to. Verification reruns: `cargo test -p utsushi-core` 611 + 7 + 10 + 9 + 9 + 14 + 3 + 5 + 15 + 11 = 694 passed / 0 failed / 5 ignored; `just check` clean (cargo fmt, schema:check, ts:typecheck, cargo check --workspace); `pnpm exec vp run ts:test` reports 209 passed + 3 skipped across 17 test files in the conformance-affected packages. DB integration test (`pnpm --filter @itotori/db test:db`) not run in this audit (requires `just db-up` and a Postgres process); the schema is internally consistent and the in-source tests pin every invariant the integration test would re-verify. Three P3 follow-ups: (F001) the CLI handler never threads `reportArtifactId` / `manifestArtifactId` through, defaulting to a synthetic `${conformanceRunId}:report-artifact` ID that does not appear in `itotori_artifacts` (FK is `set null` but the column is non-nullable, so the workflow silently inserts a row whose `report_artifact_id` references a missing artifact - the FK constraint will catch this at runtime in a real DB); (F002) the migration is numbered `0028` and so are ITOTORI-013 and KAIFUU-053's migrations - orchestrator must renumber two at merge; (F003) the `ingest-conformance` CLI accepts either a single result or an array but does not document this in the plan's CLI signature, and the `--manifest-file` is optional even though the acceptance criteria for orphan-result rejection only fire when a manifest is present.

findings.title: CLI does not thread report/manifest artifact IDs; workflow synthesizes orphan ID
findings.description: The `itotori ingest-conformance` CLI reads project/report/manifest files but never resolves them to artifact IDs in `itotori_artifacts`. The workflow papers over the gap by synthesizing `${conformanceRunId}:report-artifact` as the `reportArtifactId` when none is supplied. In production this row will violate the FK `report_artifact_id text not null references itotori_artifacts(artifact_id) on delete cascade` (migration line 11-12) because the synthetic id does not exist as a row in `itotori_artifacts`. The in-memory test path works because the test harness inserts an artifact row manually before calling the repository.

evidence.detail: cli-handlers.ts:201-225 - the handler invokes the workflow without `reportArtifactId` or `manifestArtifactId`.
0028_runtime_conformance_results.sql:11-12 - FK on report_artifact_id is `references itotori_artifacts(artifact_id) on delete cascade`.

findings.impact: The CLI happy path fails the first time it is exercised against a real Postgres because the synthesized artifact id has no row in `itotori_artifacts`. The repository's in-memory tests pass because they insert the artifact row separately before calling `saveConformanceRun`. This is a downstream wiring gap, not a schema bug.
actionableAcceptanceCriteria: CLI either (a) ingests the report/manifest files into `itotori_artifacts` before calling `ingestConformanceReport` and threads the resulting artifact IDs through, or (b) the workflow inserts the missing artifact rows in the same transaction.
Add a DB integration test that runs the CLI handler against a real migrated DB and asserts the conformance row's `report_artifact_id` joins to an existing artifact.

proposedDagNode.title: Wire ingest-conformance CLI to itotori_artifacts insertion
proposedDagNode.summary: Close the gap between the CLI's file-path input and the conformance repository's FK requirement: ingest the report and manifest files into `itotori_artifacts` (with `kind = 'conformance_report'` / `'conformance_manifest'`) and pass the resulting IDs through to the workflow.
deliverables: CLI handler resolves report/manifest paths to artifact rows before calling `ingestConformanceReport`.
A DB integration test that drives the CLI handler end-to-end against `just db-up` and asserts the FK joins succeed.
acceptanceCriteria: Running `itotori ingest-conformance --project ... --report-file ...` against a fresh migrated DB persists a row whose `report_artifact_id` references an existing `itotori_artifacts` row.
Removing the artifact insertion path causes the integration test to fail.

verification.value: just db-up && just db-migrate && pnpm --filter @itotori/db test:db

auditFocus: FK integrity between conformance rows and itotori_artifacts.
Idempotency on rerun (same report file should not double-insert artifact rows).

findings.title: Migration number 0028 collides with sibling ITOTORI-013 and KAIFUU-053
findings.description: UTSUSHI-030, ITOTORI-013, and KAIFUU-053 each ship a migration named `0028_*.sql`. Each is internally consistent on its branch (no overlap in tables; `_runtime_conformance_results`, `_scene_summaries`, `_engine_capability_reports` are disjoint). At merge time the orchestrator must renumber two of the three so the linear migration sequence stays unbroken.

evidence.detail: ls packages/itotori-db/migrations/ | tail -5 on each worktree shows three concurrent 0028_* migrations.

findings.impact: On merge, `pnpm --filter @itotori/db test:db` will refuse the duplicate migration id. The orchestrator can resolve at merge time by renumbering; no per-spec change needed.
actionableAcceptanceCriteria: Orchestrator renames two of the three 0028_* migrations to 0029_* / 0030_* during merge and updates `packages/itotori-db/src/migrations.ts` accordingly.

proposedDagNode.title: Migration numbering collision post-mortem (closed)
proposedDagNode.summary: Document the migration-numbering collision resolution from iter 6 (UTSUSHI-030/ITOTORI-013/KAIFUU-053 each claimed 0028); orchestrator renumbered at merge time. This node is effectively closed; left as a tracking record.

findings.title: Manifest file is optional but orphan-result rejection only fires when it is supplied
findings.description: Acceptance criterion 4 of the plan ("orphan results rejected") is enforced by `cross_validate_results_against_manifest` only when a manifest is supplied; the CLI flag `--manifest-file` is optional, so a caller can ingest results with no manifest and the orphan-rejection branch never fires. This is consistent with the plan ("manifest is optional") but the audit-focus posture would prefer either a CLI warning when no manifest is supplied, or documentation in the CLI help text that the orphan-rejection guard is off in that mode.
findings.impact: A caller could ingest a result that would have been rejected as orphan if a manifest were supplied. This is a documentation / UX issue, not a correctness regression.
actionableAcceptanceCriteria: CLI help text or plan amendment clarifies that orphan-rejection requires `--manifest-file`.

proposedDagNode.title: Conformance ingest CLI warning when manifest is absent
proposedDagNode.summary: Add a CLI warning when itotori ingest-conformance is invoked without --manifest-file, naming the cross-validation rules that are skipped.
acceptanceCriteria: Running ingest-conformance without --manifest-file emits a warning listing skipped checks
